INTRODUCTION
This Job Specification along with its attachments broadly describes in detail the scope
of supply & work of the Contractor, suggested execution methodology, applicable
drawings (existing and new) and standards / specifications applicable, vendor data
requirements etc. for the revamp of the existing CDU3 heater F-101 revamp for
implementation of reliability enhancement recommendations at BPCL Mumbai
refinery.

text:
1.1 The Contractor's scope includes Project management, execution, residual engineering,
detailing, fabrication drawing preparation, procurement, pre-fabrication / fabrication
(including supply of materials except for Free issue materials as covered in tender),
inspection, assembly, pre-dry-out, site erection and testing (in presence of OEM
representative / supplier for free issue / bought out items), assistance during pre-
commissioning / commissioning & PGTR etc. for the following Fired Heater and APH
System:

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > Clause 1.2 Time is the essence of this project . Revamp of the existing heater as defined in the
text:
1.2 Time is the essence of this project . Revamp of the existing heater as defined in the
subsequent clauses is to be completed in a limited period shut down. Important
contractual dates / schedules related to start / completion of work are available
elsewhere in Commercial section of the tender document . For details of facilities / utilities
if any, (e.g., construction power, water, storage space etc.) contractor to refer
commercial portion of tender.
Contractor shall accordingly consider maximum pre fabrication in modular forms
permitted as per ODC limits and ensure mobilization of resources – i.e., materials in
Contractor’s scope of supply, manpower and tools & tackles. Also, activities shall be
carried out in parallel to strictly adhere to the shutdown duration.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > Clause 1.3 Open Space for site work shall be provided by Client within refinery premises for storage
text:
1.3 Open Space for site work shall be provided by Client within refinery premises for storage
and module / assembly purposes. All pre-fabricated items / supplies shall be brought in
refinery premises in weather proof protection packing considering rain etc. Tarpaulin
wrapping shall be done for refractory lined pre dried out duct / modules. Temporary
blinding shall be done for openings to prevent ingress of water etc. Any Hydra, trailer &
crane etc. for movement / erection of modules etc. to heater location shall be in
Contractors scope if not specifically mentioned in commercial section.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > Clause 1.5 The offer submitted by the Contractor shall not contain any write-up on scope of supply
text:
1.5 The offer submitted by the Contractor shall not contain any write-up on scope of supply
/ service, scope of work, documents/drawings considered for bidding etc. which are
already covered in the tender. Any such write-up shall be ignored for the purpose of bid
evaluation. Only documents as per Vendor data requirements related to
qualification & Technical compliance are to be submitted. No other technical
details should be submitted with the Offer.
Bidder shall note that as this is a NO DEVIATION tender , therefore no technical
details are required to be submitted in the offer and by the act of submission of
bid, it is assumed & implied that Bidder’s offer is in perfect compliance to the
complete bidding document. Bidders should not bid if they have any deviation
against the tender.
1.6 For successful Contractor any deviation at the execution stage, which becomes
unavoidable, shall be immediately submitted for PMC / OWNER approval and Contractor
shall not proceed with such deviation till its acceptance by PMC / OWNER. Time taken /
delay, if any, in processing of deviation permit or its rejection shall be solely to
Contractor’s account.
It shall be the overall responsibility of the Contractor to carry out residual detailing,
fabricate, assemble, erect and test the equipment in conformity with codes /
specifications and to meet all requirements of package.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > Clause 1.7 In the event of conflict among the various documents attached with the tender document,
text:
1.7 In the event of conflict among the various documents attached with the tender document,
following order of precedence shall apply. However, as a general rule, more stringent
requirement shall govern:
(i) Statutory / Owner’s Requirements.
(ii) Heater GADs, Datasheets and P&IDs attached with the tender.
(iii) This specification and other job specifications referred herein.
(iv) EIL standards & Standard specifications.
(v) Codes and Standards.
However, all such conflicts shall be referred to PMC / OWNER for clarification at the
earliest and the decision of PMC / OWNER shall be final and binding on the Contractor
without any cost and delivery implications.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > Clause 1.10 To facilitate site erection, the fabrication shall be done in modules & assemblies
text:
1.10 To facilitate site erection, the fabrication shall be done in modules & assemblies
prefabricated in the Vendor’s shop in line with modularization scheme reflected in the
tender drawings / documents, so as to minimize the site work during the shutdown
period. The erection modus operandi to be adopted by the Contractor shall be highlighted
in the offer in detail along with bar charts. All balance activities are to be finished in the
shutdown period specified.

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > Clause 1.11 Contractor shall consider site conditions, approach roads, cable trench, drains, space
text:
1.11 Contractor shall consider site conditions, approach roads, cable trench, drains, space
availability, utility lines battery limit and all issues enabling the erection of the items
covered in the Scope of work. Clarifications, if any, shall be obtained before submitting
his bids. No such issue will be entertained during the execution stage for compensations.
Contractor shall be asked to give detailed presentation on modular construction /
execution methodology before / after award of job and all Owner / EIL comments shall
be incorporated.

text:
1.16.1 Contractor or his Sub- contractor should have executed “Coil Fabrication” of the coil
metallurgies applicable for job or higher materials (Refer Clause 1.16.2 for Metallurgy
groupings) in at least one Fired Heater / Boiler / any other Fired Equipment / Waste Heat
Recovery System / Piping in a hydrocarbon service as covered by IBR / ASME Sec. I,
VIII or B31.3 in last ten years. This job should be in operation for a minimum of one year

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > 1.16 EXPERIENCE CRITERIA FOR ACTIVITIES: > Clause 1.16.2 Metallurgy grouping - The coil materials are grouped as per following hierarchy (from
text:
1.16.2 Metallurgy grouping - The coil materials are grouped as per following hierarchy (from
lower to higher):
a) CS
b) Low Alloy steel (LAS) up to P11
c) P22
d) P5 / P9 / P91
e) SS
f) High Ni alloys like HP / Incoloy etc.
Experience of any metallurgy within the group shall qualify for all other metallurgies in
the group.
Experience in lower coil metallurgy group shall not be considered for qualifying to a
higher metallurgy group, however vice versa shall be acceptable.

1.23 Since during pre-shut down (PSD) the revamp activities are to be taken up in a running
plant, all necessary barricading, safety related precautions, tools & tackles shall be the
Contractor’s responsibility. All the OWNER’s safety recommendations, HSE
requirements and all applicable rules & regulations shall be complied to by the
Contractor.
Contractor to note that during Pre shut down, hot work may be permitted with compliance
of safety requirements / local barricading isolation. Owner’s approval for the same shall
be obtained before proceeding for hot work.
1.24 Inspection at contractor’s shop and within refinery premise to be carried out by TPI
engaged by the contractor at his own cost. This includes all the inspection activities
during the Pre TA & TA. The TPI agency engaged will be as per the approved TPI list
from BPCL/EIL. List of Inspection agencies for TPI is attached in commercial portion of
tender.

with respect to quality & quantity of the same. Any discrepancy must be brought to the
notice of the owner at the earliest possible so that the overall completion schedule is not
affected. Contractor to ensure proper covering of all FIMs to save it from rain/water
ingress using suitable means (tarpaulin/plastic cover) arranged by contractor at no extra
cost.
In case of any kind of damage to the free issued items (once handed over and accepted
by contractor) on account of improper handling, storage etc. which makes it unfit for use
in its intended purpose as per specification and requirements, shall be repair / rectified /
replaced by Contractor without time and cost implication to Owner.

1.30 Complete utility piping items routing modification (as applicable on account of APH
replacement / other local modifications in existing connections) including Hookup with
existing lines, supply, fabrication, supporting, testing and installation is in contractor
scope. Isometrics drawings for the same are to be prepared by Contractor and submitted
for Owner / EIL approval after award. Contractor to obtain further clarity for battery limit
during site visit.
1.31 Inspection Record Documentation
Erection of the equipment shall be done as per the OEM procedures and compliance to
the permitted tolerances as defined in supplier specifications / tender specifications.
The details of all such records shall be maintained and shall be produced to Owner /

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > 2.0 EQUIPMENT SUMMARY > 2.1 Existing Heater system

breadcrumb: JOB SPECIFICATION, SCOPE OF SUPPLY & WORK FOR CDU3 F-101 HEATER REVAMP CDU3 F-101 REVAMP FOR IMPLEMENTATION OF > 2.0 EQUIPMENT SUMMARY > 2.1 Existing Heater system
text:
The existing heater is a Multicellular box type Vertical tube heater. There is total of 272
tubes in the radiant section distributed in 8 fluid passes and 4 cells (without any wall
partition). The convection section is located on top of the radiant section.
Each radiant cell has 6 floor mounted dual fired (Fuel oil, Fuel gas and off-gas) burners,
i.e. total 24 burners in 4 cells, arranged in a staggered fashion.
The heater has a balanced draft system (Outboard Cast and glass type APH with two
FD fans and one ID fan). The heater shares a common structure mounted stack with the
adjoining VDU heater.

text: In brief the purpose of the revamp is: - - Increasing the number of tubes per pass in radiant section by adding 8 nos. of new 6” radiant tubes per pass. - Addition of 2 row of tubes (8 tubes per row) in the existing future tube rows of the convection section. - Replacement of existing Cast & glass type APH with latest design CAPH & GAPH (as per new APH datasheet) for increased thermal duty and reliability. - Replacing all existing 24 nos. of oil guns with new oil guns. - Associated modifications in radiant coil / cross over / terminals / spigots, tube supports, radiant casing / floor / arch and refractory modifications. - Associated APH system modification including APH support structure modification. - Associated convection section / convection coil / convection terminal modification. - Associated skin thermocouple modification & other instrument related modification. - Relocation / replacement of radiant inlet / outlet sleeves as per new arrangement and making provision for installation of tube supports (wall tube hangers & top supports) with necessary steel (floor, wall & arch) modifications. - Bolts / washers as required for installation of tube supports need to be procured by contractor in adequate length and numbers including for re used supports considering revamp. Heater contractor is responsible on single point basis for completing all activities defined in tender for the revamp of the heater. For detailed scope of supply and work, contractor

text:
3.3.4 Post shutdown activities: Any balance work from pre-shutdown and shutdown
activities due to any unforeseen situation, to be executed by contractor post shutdown
without any extra cost. Contractor to ensure completion of all activities during pre-
shutdown and shutdown period.
The Contractor shall consider and prepare a detailed methodology, aligned with their
APH procurement schedule, for replacement of the existing APH either during the
shutdown or subsequent to the shutdown with the heater system operating in FD mode.
In case the APH replacement is required to be executed with the heater operating in FD
mode, all system- enabling activities during the shutdown shall be within the Contractor’s
scope.
Redundant isolation of the APH system in the flue-gas path and air path, in addition to
isolation guillotine dampers and control dampers, shall be implemented during the
shutdown itself.

Apart from final hydro testing of assembled radiant and convection tubes, each
convection studded tubes shall be hydro tested at ground after welding of bridging
pieces, 50 mm extra length may be considered on each side of tube for hydro testing
which shall be cut to final length as per drawing. Complete water removal after hydro

coils are not possible, Contractor may propose alternatives to hydro-testing subject to
Owner / EIL approval.

defective design and materials, poor workmanship and failure from normal usage for a
period as specified in tender document / GPC. Supplier shall repair or replace, without
charge any defective parts found within that time. This guarantee shall also include sub-
ordered / subcontracted / bought-out items forming part of the total contractor supplies.
If a separate guarantee clause has been specified in procurement specification for items
in Contractor scope of supply, then the stringent of the two shall govern.

text:
7.5 During performance guarantee test run (PGTR) at site, Heater contractor with support
from EIL shall demonstrate the guaranteed performance parameters corresponding to
the Heater design load (77.0 MMKcal / hr) and fuel efficiency (guaranteed min. 90.4% in
fuel oil firing) mentioned in the API datasheet. In an event that unit / CDU-3 Crude heater
operates at a throughput lower than the heater design throughput / condition, the
guaranteed performance parameters (load and fuel efficiency as mentioned above) of
the heater shall be demonstrated for the purpose of guarantee in mutual discussion with
BPCL. PGTR procedure shall be finalized between BPCL and EIL.

Three sets of “As built” drawings and “Data folder” (containing test certificates, inspection
reports etc.), including one set of reproducible and electronic formats, shall be supplied
by the successful bidder along with the equipment. Soft copies of all drawings in
AutoCAD of version 2008 or later (.DWG format) shall be submitted to BPCL in DVD/Pen

including the “AS BUILT DRAWING” are updated & complete in all Design
Engineering respects and shall incorporate all suggestions/correction informed by BPCL
or the authorized inspector while obtaining certificate of completeness from the
Inspection Agency.
